probes of $LOCK_FILE @LOCK_PROBES = (1, 2, 4, 8, 16); # Maximum time allowed on grading lock in seconds. $LOCK_EXPIRATION = 300; # Maximum time allowed for grading one submission before run-tests warns # of expired locks, in seconds. $MAX_GRADING_

turns a UNIX time value for the local time TIME, if it is a valid # time string having the format "mm/dd/yyyy", "mm/dd/yyyy:hh:mm", # "mm/dd/yyyy:hhmm", "mm/dd/yyyy:hh:mm:ss", or "mm/dd/yyyy:hhmmss". # Otherwise returns the undefined value

hmmss". # Otherwise returns the undefined value. sub ToTime { my $time = shift; my ($mon,$day,$year,$hour,$min, $sec, $hour2, $min2, $sec2) = ($time =~ m=([0-9]{1,2})/([0-9]{1,2})/([0-9]{4})(?::([0-9]{1,2}):([0-9]{1,2})(?::([0-9]{1,

) { $hour = 23; $min = 59; } if (! defined ($sec)) { $sec = 0; } if (!defined ($mon) || $mon > 12 || $mon == 0 || $day == 0 || $day > 31 || $hour > 23 || $min > 59) { return undef; } return timelocal($sec, $min, $hour, $d

; } return timelocal($sec, $min, $hour, $day, $mon-1, $year); } # Usage: FileTime FILENAME # Return mtime of FILENAME. sub FileTime { my $fileName = shift; return (stat ($fileName))[9]; } # Usage: TimeStampToTime TIME # Given a

[9]; } # Usage: TimeStampToTime TIME # Given a timestamp (from a Message-Id), return a UNIX time. sub TimeStampToTime { my ($year, $mon, $day, $hour, $min, $sec) = ($_[0] =~ /(....)(..)(..)(..)(..)(..)?/); $sec = 0 if (not defined ($sec)

.)(..)(..)?/); $sec = 0 if (not defined ($sec)); return timegm ($sec, $min, $hour, $day, $mon-1, $year); } # Usage: TimeToTimeStamp UNIXTIME # Given a UNIX time, return a timestamp as from a Message-Id, format # YYYYMMDDHHMM, in UTC.

rom a Message-Id, format # YYYYMMDDHHMM, in UTC. sub TimeToTimeStamp { my $time = shift; my ($sec,$min,$hour,$mday,$mon,$year,$wday,$yday,$isdst) = gmtime ($time); return sprintf ("%04d%02d%02d%02d%02d", 1900+$year,

02d%02d%02d%02d", 1900+$year, $mon+1, $mday, $hour, $min); } ### # Messages ### # Usage: Fatal MSG sub Fatal { my $msg = shift; print REAL_STDERR "$msg\n"; &CleanUp exit 2; } # Usage: Warn MSG sub Warn { my

exit 2; } # Usage: Warn MSG sub Warn { my $msg = shift; print REAL_STDERR "Warning: $msg\n"; } # Usage: Note MSG sub Note { my $msg = shift; print REAL_STDERR "$msg\n"; } ### # RE utilities ### # Usage: CheckPatn PATN # Tru

RE utilities ### # Usage: CheckPatn PATN # True if /PATN/ is a valid regular expression. sub CheckPatn { return eval (/$_[0]/ || 1); } ### # System commands/info. ### # Usage: System CMND # Execute CMND as for 'system', returning true f

Execute CMND as for 'system', returning true for 0 exit code. sub System { return (system (@_) == 0); } sub MyLogin { return (getpwuid($<))[0] || Fatal ("Can't tell who I am."); } sub CurrentDir { my $pwd = `pwd`; chomp $pwd;

rrentDir { my $pwd = `pwd`; chomp $pwd; return $pwd; } sub Lock { my ($probe, $try, @stats, $mkdir_ok); return if ($have_lock); my $old_umask = umask (); for ($try = 0; $try < 2; $try += 1) { for ($probe = 0; $probe <= $

ry < 2; $try += 1) { for ($probe = 0; $probe <= $#LOCK_PROBES; $probe += 1) { umask (0); $mkdir_ok = mkdir ("$LOCK_FILE", 0777); umask ($old_umask); if ($mkdir_ok) { $have_lock = 1; return 1; } sleep $LOCK_PROBES[$p

= 1; return 1; } sleep $LOCK_PROBES[$probe]; } @stats = stat $LOCK_FILE; if ($#stats > 0) { if (time() - $stats[10] > $LOCK_EXPIRATION) { if (Yorn ("Lock busy, but expired. Shall I break it?")) { rmdir $LOCK_FILE; }

l I break it?")) { rmdir $LOCK_FILE; } } else { die "Grading files busy. Please try later.\n"; } } } die "Grading files busy. Please try later.\n"; } sub Unlock { if ($have_lock) { $have_lock = 0; rmdir $LOCK_FILE;

($have_lock) { $have_lock = 0; rmdir $LOCK_FILE; } } # Protect BLOCK_REFERENCE # Mask selected signals temporarily and execute BLOCK_REFERENCE sub Protect { local $SIG{HUP} = "IGNORE"; local $SIG{INT} = "IGNORE"; local $SIG{QUIT} =

local $SIG{INT} = "IGNORE"; local $SIG{QUIT} = "IGNORE"; local $SIG{TERM} = "IGNORE"; local $SIG{TSTP} = "IGNORE"; &{$_[0]}(); } # GetPermissions @COMMAND # If not currently running with class-master effective ID, seek to # run COMMA

ith class-master effective ID, seek to # run COMMAND with class permissions. sub GetPermissions { if ($> != (stat ($ENV{"MASTERDIR"}))[4]) { exec "$MASTER_PROGRAM", @_; die "Could not execute $MASTER_PROGRAM."; } } # GLOB PATHPATN # Array

characters are in the last component of the path. # [It's here only because glob blows up on large results, and fails # with an error message when a directory in PATHPATN does not exist # (in the latter case, we sensibly return the empty array).] s

tter case, we sensibly return the empty array).] sub GLOB { my ($dir0,$base) = ($_[0] =~ m{ ^(.*/)?([^/]*)$ }x); my $dir = $dir0; if ($dir eq "") { $dir = "./"; } $base =~ s/\./\\./g; $base =~ s/\*/.*/g; $base =~ s/\

\./\\./g; $base =~ s/\*/.*/g; $base =~ s/\?/./g; chop $dir; opendir (GLOBDIR, $dir) || return (); my @result; if ($base =~ /^\\\./) { @result = grep { /^$base$/ } readdir (GLOBDIR); } else { @result = grep { /^$base$/ a

OBDIR); } else { @result = grep { /^$base$/ and /^[^.]/ } readdir (GLOBDIR); } closedir GLOBDIR; foreach (@result) { $_ = "$dir0$_"; } return sort (@result); } ### # Interactive responses ### # Usage: Yorn MSG [DEFAULT]

active responses ### # Usage: Yorn MSG [DEFAULT] # Read a yes/no response from STDIN, prompting with MSG. Return true # iff answer is yes. sub Yorn { my $msg = shift; my $dflt; $dflt = 0; if ($#_ >= 0) { $dflt = shif
dflt = 0; if ($#_ >= 0) { $dflt = shift; } while (1) { if (defined($msg)) { print STDERR "$msg [yes/no] "; } $_ = ; if (not defined $_) { return $dflt; } /^y(es)?$/i && do

return $dflt; } /^y(es)?$/i && do { return 1; }; /^no?$/i && do { return 0; }; print STDERR "Please answer 'yes' or 'no'\n"; } } # Usage: Fetch MSG # Read a non-empty line from STDIN, prompting with MSG. sub Fetch { my

rom STDIN, prompting with MSG. sub Fetch { my $msg = shift; my $result; $result = ""; while ($result eq "") { print STDERR "$msg"; $result = ; if (! defined($result)) { return $result; } $result =~ s/^\s

return $result; } $result =~ s/^\s*//; $result =~ s/\s*$//; } return $result; } # Usage: Fetch0 MSG # Read a line (possibly empty) from STDIN, prompting with MSG. sub Fetch0 { my $msg = shift; my $result; pri

e: StudentExists LOGIN # True iff LOGIN is a student registered in this class. sub StudentExists { my ($login) = @_; return (-e "$REGISTER_DIR/$login") ? 1 : 0; } # Usage: TeamExists TEAM # True iff TEAM is a registered team name. sub

# True iff TEAM is a registered team name. sub TeamExists { my ($team) = @_; return (-e "$TEAM_DIR/$team") ? 1 : 0; } # Usage: TeamMembers TEAM # A list of the members of TEAM as a whitespace-separated string. sub TeamMembers { my

ck exit code for now. close (TEAM); $line =~ s/^.*?:\s*//; return $line; } # Usage: GetCode LOGIN # The codeword supplied by LOGIN during registration sub GetCode { my ($login) = @_; open (REGISTRATION, "<$REGISTER_DIR/$login"

?$login?"; } # Usage: AssignmentExists ASSIGN # True iff ASSIGN is listed among the recognized assignment names. sub AssignmentExists { my ($assign) = @_; if ($assign eq "dummy") { return 1; } if ($assign eq "lab11") { return 1;

the form # .../LOGIN.TIMESTAMP) as used in the SUBMISSION_DIR directory. This orderimg # is by LOGIN and then either by TIMESTAMP, or, if both denote GIT submissions, # by submit tag sequence numbers. sub SubmissionOrder($$) { my ($subm1, $subm2

issions to references to lists # of logins of submitters. Here, a "submission" is of the form # " . ". sub SubmissionMap { my $assgn = shift; my $dir = "$SUBMISSION_DIR/$assgn"; my ($result, $login, $realSubm); if (not opendi

y ($result, $login, $realSubm); if (not opendir (SUBMISSIONS, $dir)) { return { }; } $result = { }; my @allSubmissions = readdir (SUBMISSIONS); closedir (SUBMISSIONS); foreach my $subm (@allSubmissions) { next if $subm !~ /^

my $subm (@allSubmissions) { next if $subm !~ /^(\S+)\.\d+$/; $login = $1; $f = "$dir/$subm"; $realSubm = RealSubmission ($f); next if $realSubm !~ /^(\S+)\.\d+$/; $$result{$login} = ($$result{$login} or []); $$result{$realSubm} = ($$result{$

by spaces. sub Partnerships { my $map = shift; my $login = shift; my %partnerships; my $submissions = $$map{$login}; if ($submissions) { foreach my $subm (@$submissions) { $partnerships{join (' ', sort (@{$$map{$subm}}))} =

partnerships{join (' ', sort (@{$$map{$subm}}))} = 1; } } return keys (%partnerships); } # Usage IsPartnerSubmission FILE # True iff FILE represents a submission by a partner. FILE must be the # pathname of a file under the submissi

ning the actual submission for the # submission contained in FILE. This is FILE itself, unless it represents # a partner's entry, in which case it is the name under which the project # was actually submitted. sub RealSubmission { my $f

actually submitted. sub RealSubmission { my $file = shift; my $base; if ($base = readlink ($file)) { return $base; } else { $file =~ m{.*/(.*)}; return $1; } } # Usage GetDeadlineExceptions # A hash mapping ASSGN/LOGIN and

ineExceptions # A hash mapping ASSGN/LOGIN and ASSGN/TEAM pairs to deadlines specific # to those pairs, as indicated by the deadline-exceptions file. If # WARN, print warnings of anomalies, otherwise ignore. sub GetDeadlineExceptions {

, otherwise ignore. sub GetDeadlineExceptions { my $warn = shift; my %lateException; open (EXCEPTIONS, $DEADLINE_EXCEPTIONS) or return (); while ( ) { if (my ($assgn, $id, $time) = /^\s*([^\s#]\S*)\s+(\S+)\s+(\S+)/) { if (not As

Invalid time ($3) in lateness exception file.") if $warn; next; } $lateException{"$assgn/$id"} = ToTime ($time); } } close EXCEPTIONS; return %lateException; } ### # Utilities for initialization ### # Usage: InitDir DIR MODE

for initialization ### # Usage: InitDir DIR MODE [ GROUP-ID ] sub InitDir { my ($dir) = shift; my ($mode) = shift; my ($group) = shift; my ($old_umask) = umask (); my ($dev,$ino,$omode,$nlink,$uid,$ogid,$rdev,$size) = stat $dir;

$omode,$nlink,$uid,$ogid,$rdev,$size) = stat $dir; if (not defined $uid) { umask 0; mkdir ($dir, $mode) or Warn ("Could not create directory $dir."); umask ($old_umask); } if (defined ($group)) { $gid = scalar (getgrnam ($group));

($group)) { $gid = scalar (getgrnam ($group)); if ($gid != $ogid and chown ($>, $gid, $dir) != 1) { Warn ("Could not set group of $dir to $group."); return 0; }; } if ($mode != ($omode & 03777) and chmod ($mode, $dir) != 1) { W

$omode & 03777) and chmod ($mode, $dir) != 1) { Warn ("Could not set mode of $dir to $mode."); return 0; }; return 1; } # Usage: ShellQuote ARG # Returns ARG quoted in such a way that when interpolated into a shell command, # it will denot

interpolated into a shell command, # it will denote the original value of ARG. sub ShellQuote { my $arg = shift; $arg =~ s/([\$"`\\])/\\$1/g; return "\"$arg\""; } ### # Clean up ### undef %CLEAN_ME; sub Clean1 { my $file = shift;
